question:
What is the underlying ethical principle of the "Golden Rule" (treat others as you want to be treated)?
A) It promotes fundamental empathy, asking individuals to recognize the shared humanity in others.
B) It requires everyone to buy each other gold jewelry.
C) It assumes everyone has the exact same hobbies and preferences.
D) It encourages people to ignore others completely.

response.text:
The Golden Rule, while seemingly straightforward, operates on the assumption that everyone desires the same treatment. Experts often point out that this can be flawed because people have diverse needs, preferences, and values. Applying your own desires universally can be insensitive or even harmful if you don't consider individual differences. Therefore, the underlying assumption of shared preferences (C) is a key point of discussion and critique regarding the Golden Rule.
Answer: C
